file_name: EMERALDHEALTHTHERAPEUTICSINC_06_10_2020-EX-4.5-CONSULTING AGREEMENT - DR. GAETANO MORELLO N.D. INC..txt

perturbation.original_text: 2.3 Reporting and Oversight Responsibility. The Contractor will report to and take instructions from Avtar Dhillon, President and Executive Chair.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 Reporting and Oversight Responsibility. The Consultant will report to and take instructions from Avtar Dhillon, President and Executive Chair.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Contractor' to 'Consultant' introduces ambiguity as 'Contractor' is a defined term, but 'Consultant' is not, potentially creating uncertainty about who Avtar Dhillon provides oversight to, creating in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Position. The Company hereby engages the Contractor as an independent contractor and the Contractor hereby agrees to such engagement.
perturbation.justification: The original contract defines the role as 'Contractor' in section 2.1. The change in section 2.3 from 'Contractor' to 'Consultant' creates a contradiction because the contract explicitly defines 'Contractor,' creating confusion about who Avtar Dhillon provides oversight to. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation, where using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations. The contract is now unclear on whether Avtar Dhillon provides oversight to the 'Contractor' or the ambiguously introduced 'Consultant,' creating an in-text contradiction and uncertainty regarding contractual obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Fee. The Company will pay to the Contractor an annual fee (the "Fee") of $240,000. per year plus plus GST and applicable taxes, if any.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Fee. The Company will pay to the Specialist an annual fee (the "Fee") of $240,000. per year plus plus GST and applicable taxes, if any.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Contractor' to 'Specialist' introduces ambiguity, since 'Contractor' is defined as Dr. Gaetano Morello N.D. INC. and this changes the payee to an undefined term called 'Specialist', making the payment obligation uncertain, and creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Position. The Company hereby engages the Contractor as an independent contractor and the Contractor hereby agrees to such engagement.
perturbation.justification: The original text identifies 'Contractor' (defined elsewhere as Dr. Gaetano Morello N.D. INC.) as the payee. The changed text replaces 'Contractor' with 'Specialist', which is an undefined term. This creates a contradiction because Section 2.1 explicitly states the contract is with 'the Contractor' as an independent contractor, thus obligating payment to the 'Contractor'. Changing the payee in Section 3.1 to an undefined 'Specialist' introduces ambiguity and makes it unclear who is entitled to the fee, conflicting with the explicit engagement in Section 2.1. This misalignment of terminology directly results in an in-text contradiction as it pertains to payment obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2 Contractor's Right to Terminate Agreement for any Reason. The Contractor may terminate this Agreement and his or her engagement for any reason at any time upon providing 30 days advance notice in writing to Emerald.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 The Expert's Right to Terminate Agreement for any Reason. The Expert may terminate this Agreement and his or her engagement for any reason at any time upon providing 30 days advance notice in writing to Emerald.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Contractor' with 'The Expert' confuses the right to terminate. The contract defines rights for the 'Contractor'. Making the 'Expert' the terminating party leads to confusion as the expert is not defined and the contractor is. Introducing in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3 Emeralds' Right to Terminate this Agreement for Cause. The Company may terminate this Agreement and the Contractor's engagement for Cause at any time on written notice to the Contractor. The date of termination will be the date specified in the written notice and may be, in the sole discretion of the Company, the same day the notice is given to the Contractor, or such later date as the Company may decide.
perturbation.justification: The original contract refers to 'Contractor' as the entity with the right to terminate in Section 5.2. The changed text replaces 'Contractor' with 'The Expert.' Section 5.3 explicitly grants Emerald the right to terminate the 'Contractor's' engagement. By introducing 'The Expert' as the terminating party in 5.2, the contract creates ambiguity and a direct contradiction regarding who has the right to terminate the agreement and whose engagement Emerald can terminate, as the 'Expert' is not defined elsewhere. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology leading to in-text contradictions.
